items.title: Two trackers
items.body: This app has two separate trackers: Habits and Pills. They use similar screens and reminders, but the counting logic is different.

items.title: Edits don’t rewrite everything
items.body: Most edits update the current setup going forward. The app does not automatically rewrite all past history when you change a schedule later.

items.title: Lists stay separate
items.body: Habits and Pills are stored and managed separately. Changes in one tab are not supposed to affect the other.

items.title: Habit types
items.body: Habits can be created as either Build Habit or Quit Habit. This affects how the habit is categorized, but tracking still happens by marking days as completed.

items.title: Marking progress
items.body: A habit can be marked completed for today, or the mark can be removed. This can be done from the list or by editing recent history.

items.title: Editing history
items.body: Habit history can only be edited for the recent editable window. Older history stays visible, but it is not meant to be changed from edit.

items.title: Schedule changes
items.body: If you change a habit schedule later, the app keeps the historical record that already exists. It does not fully recalculate older completions from scratch.

items.title: Start date creates the base history
items.body: When a pill is created, the app uses the Start Date to generate the initial taken history automatically.

items.title: Use schedule for history
items.body: If enabled, only scheduled days are counted from the start date up to yesterday. If disabled, every day from the start date up to yesterday is counted.

items.title: Today is not auto-counted on create
items.body: Initial pill history is generated only up to yesterday. Creating a pill today does not automatically mark it as taken today.

items.title: Editing recent pill history
items.body: Pill history can be corrected only for the recent editable window. Older history stays stored for totals and details, but it is not directly rewritten in edit.

items.title: Pills use total days
items.body: Pills do not use streaks. They only track the total number of days marked as taken.

items.title: Permission is requested during setup
items.body: The app asks for notification permission during app setup so reminders can be available immediately.

items.title: Reminder window is limited
items.body: The app schedules reminders only for a short upcoming window, currently the next 2 days. This helps avoid system notification limits.

items.title: Grouped reminders
items.body: If multiple reminders fall at the same time, the app may group them into a single notification instead of showing one per item.

items.title: Reminders refresh often
items.body: Reminder scheduling is refreshed when the app becomes active and after reminder-related changes such as create, edit, delete, mark, or unmark.

items.title: Habits use streaks
items.body: Habits track current streak, longest streak, and completion history.

items.title: Pills use total days only
items.body: Pills track only total days taken. Missed days do not break a streak because pills do not use streak logic.

items.title: History windows are different
items.body: Habits and Pills do not use the same history rules everywhere. Create flow, edit flow, and details screens may use different history windows depending on the feature.

items.title: Older history stays visible
items.body: Even when older history is not editable, it still contributes to totals and still appears in the visual history where supported.

items.title: Backup stores both trackers
items.body: Backup includes both Habits and Pills, including their schedules, reminder settings, and stored history.

items.title: Restore replaces app data
items.body: When a backup is restored, the app rebuilds the stored data from that backup. This can change what appears in both trackers.

items.title: Notifications are rebuilt after restore
items.body: After restore, reminder notifications are rebuilt from the restored data so reminders match the current stored state.

items.title: Use backup for manual correction if needed
items.body: If something was entered incorrectly a long time ago, backup data can be a practical way to inspect or correct older stored records.
